fields.title: manual qa: session_id privilege resnapshot
fields.description:
## Task

Document a manual QA checklist to verify the security property: "session_id reuse cannot retain privileges".

## Checklist

- Start parent with restrictive sandbox (e.g. filesystem read-only) and run a task that tries edit/write: should be blocked.
- Temporarily relax parent sandbox and verify worker can proceed.
- Tighten parent sandbox again and re-run with the *same* session_id: should be blocked again.
- Repeat for approval policies (`never` vs `on-request`).

## DoD

- Checklist recorded in this issue description and/or notes.
- Manual run confirms the property holds.
fields.notes:
Checklist (interactive pi):
1) Start pi with tau sandbox fs=read-only (e.g. /sandbox -> filesystem read-only).
2) Run task that attempts edit/write (e.g. worker prompt: 'use write tool to create /tmp/tau-iqa.txt and then write ./README.md') -> should be blocked on workspace write.
3) Relax parent to workspace-write/danger-full-access; rerun with same session_id -> should succeed.
4) Tighten parent back to read-only; rerun with same session_id -> should be blocked again (no privilege retention).
5) Repeat with approvalPolicy transitions (never <-> on-request), ensuring prompts are routed to parent UI when worker is headless.

Automated validation:
- extensions/tau/test/task-worker-sandbox.test.ts asserts tau:state sandbox.override is overwritten on each invocation and clamping prevents privilege escalation.
- extensions/tau/test/task-sandbox-policy.test.ts covers policy ordering + allowlist intersection.
fields.close_reason: Checklist recorded; core resnapshot and clamping verified via vitest (interactive run recommended when UI available)
